description_general: Production of Lindner steel pedestals — raw material extraction through manufacturing at Arnstorf plant.
description_a1: Raw material supply: galvanised steel (>98%). Steel sheets contain 18.2% and rolled steel 26.6% secondary (recycled) material. Locking glue, thread sealant and EVA gaskets make up <0.5% each.
description_a2: Transport of raw materials to factory gate, including internal logistics. Based on typical distances and transport modes.
description_a3: Manufacturing at Lindner Arnstorf plant: stamping, forming, welding, galvanising. Powered by electricity and compressed air. GWP electricity mix: 0.67 kg CO2e/kWh; thermal energy: 0.223 kg CO2e/kWh.

description_general: Transport of steel pedestals from factory gate to construction site.
description_s1: Transport distance 100 km by diesel truck. Fuel use: 0.0437 l/100km.

description_general: Installation on site — low environmental impact. Only packaging disposal is accounted for.
description_s1: Packaging waste: 0.0366 kg per declared unit. Wooden pallets and cardboard can be reused or recycled. No significant energy or emissions from installation itself.

description_general: Deconstruction and dismantling at end of life.
description_s1: Trained personnel dismantle pedestals on site to ensure maximum recyclability. Steel components are separated for recycling. Low energy input due to modular and adjustable system design.

description_general: Transport from deconstruction site to recycling facility.
description_s1: Steel transported 50 km to nearest recycling facility by diesel truck.

95% of steel pedestal reaches end-of-waste status and is sent for recycling. Adhesive and plastic gaskets are incinerated (no recycling credit for these in Module D). GWP-total C3: 1.69E-01 kg CO2eq/m².
5% of non-recyclable material directed to landfill (0.065 kg per declared unit). GWP-total C4: 9.91E-04 kg CO2eq/m².

description_general: Reuse / recovery / recycling potential beyond system boundary.
description_s1: Credit from recycling of net scrap steel substituting primary steel production at an external facility. Negative value indicates a carbon benefit. GWP-total D: -2.02 kg CO2eq/m². Benefits reflect energy savings and reduced raw material consumption from high steel recyclability.
